summary: Mojave 10.14 right click drop down menus not working correctly
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.0; rv:52.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/52.0 Steps to reproduce: I'm entering this bug on behalf of see forum question: tubascuba https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1235792# quoted info: Thunderbird 52.9.1 installed with Mojave 10.14 when trying to move, copy, mark etc. an email using the right click on an email menu the drop downs soon stop working so not allowing the next part of the drop down menu to function. A simple quit and restart solves it but the problem soon returns. The same problem is present if I restart Thunderbird in Safe Mode. I have deleted the plist file and restarted but that didn't work either. Actual results: E.g. if I try to move an email I often get as far as say Local Folders and then the next menu does not drop down. So cannot select where to move to. Expected results: All drop down menus to open and any subset drop down menu, allowing selection. I've asked the user to try version 60 to see if same problem occurs.

summary: Thunderbird Locks up if it can't reach an IMAP Server
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_14_5) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/75.0.3770.142 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Tried to open my Unified Inbox while a mail server was down. Actual results: Thunderbird locked up and had to be force quit. Expected results: Thunderbird should have ignored the mail server and moved on to the next or alerted me that one server was down and then moved on to the next.

summary: Pressing `Enter` on message list column picker opens selected message instead
bug_report_text:
+++ This bug was initially created as a clone of Bug #1829095 +++

Pressing `Enter` on message list column picker opens selected message instead when it should just open the column picker menu.
Seen on Daily 114.0a1 (2023-04-19) (64-bit), Win10. 

STR
- Select a message in message list, then focus 
- Press `Shift`+`Tab` to focus the little column picker icon (going backwards, we can currently avoid bug 1829095)
- Press `Enter` on focused column picker

Actual
- selected message opens in a tab

Expected
- Column picker menu should open (as it does when you press `Space` on the icon)

summary: Thunderbird macOS code signature has invalid sealed resources
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_14) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/12.0 Safari/605.1.15 Steps to reproduce: 1. Download the latest macOS release (60) and mount the downloaded disk image 2. Verify the app bundle code signature with `codesign` and `--deep` option which verifies all subcomponents: ``` $ codesign --verify --verbose --deep /Volumes/Thunderbird/Thunderbird.app /Volumes/Thunderbird/Thunderbird.app/: a sealed resource is missing or invalid In subcomponent: /Volumes/Thunderbird/Thunderbird.app/Contents/Library/Spotlight/thunderbird.mdimporter file added: /Volumes/Thunderbird/Thunderbird.app/Contents/Library/Spotlight/thunderbird.mdimporter/Contents/._Info.plist file added: /Volumes/Thunderbird/Thunderbird.app/Contents/Library/Spotlight/thunderbird.mdimporter/Contents/Resources/._schema.xml file added: /Volumes/Thunderbird/Thunderbird.app/Contents/Library/Spotlight/thunderbird.mdimporter/Contents/Resources/English.lproj/._InfoPlist.strings file added: /Volumes/Thunderbird/Thunderbird.app/Contents/Library/Spotlight/thunderbird.mdimporter/Contents/Resources/English.lproj/._schema.strings ``` Expected results: The code signature should be valid including all subcomponents

summary: E-mail has disappeared in migration from Windows 7 to Windows 10
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/78.0.3904.108 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: I moved a profile from Windows 7 to Windows 10. Moved the "Thunderbird" folder from AppData/Roaming on 7 to AppData/Roaming on Win 10. I deleted the *.msf files (while Thunderbird was not running) and did a repair folder. Actual results: The profile and all configuration comes up. BUT!! My inbox in an account (not the Local Folders) is empty. Other folders don't show all the messages (only some). Oddly enough, Trash does show all messages. I've looked at the files in a text editor. All messages are there. Expected results: I should have seen all the e-mail in existing folders.

summary: changing network setting fails in Apple menu
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.13; rv:62.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/62.0 Steps to reproduce: 1. using Thunderbird 52.9.1 on macOS High Sierra 10.13.6 2. Thunderbird window is top on Finder and active 3. change network setting through AppleMenu (i.e. Applemenu -> Location -> other location) Actual results: Same as bug 1362238 Network profile remains the same location, nothing happens in network setting. Mozilla crash reporter window opens, but it appears just behind the Thunderbird's window, so hardly recodnized. Thunderbird does not freeze. Can continue using Thunderbird. Every attempt to change location results opening crash reporter windows. So multiple crash window open behind the browser window. Expected results: Crash reporter window does not open. Network setting changes normaly.

summary: TB crashes during opening s/mime signed mail from mailman mailinglist
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9122597 TB-Crash-Fwd_attachment_both_signed.eml User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/79.0.3945.130 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Symphtom: ========== Forward a S/MIME signed mail to a mailman mailinglist AND signe it with S/MIME again and send the forwarded mail as attachment. Now open the mail and Thunderbird crashes. I don't know if this problem causes other security risks. Unless you have checked this I would mark this problem as harmful security problem. Feel free to "unckeck" this and open this bug for world access/read. Affected TB versions round about above v60. TB crashes on Linux and Windows, too. Other architectures not tested. summary: no emails shown
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9047455 empty window Daily 0227 was fine. Today, tried Daily 0228: no emails are shown, for any account or folder. Just a blank window. See attachment "empty window". This occurs across multiple IMAP accounts, and all folders within those accounts. Note that accounts & folders are still shown correctly on the LHS, but selecting them makes no difference. … Worse, I'm now stuffed because I can't go back to Daily 0227: "you've launched an older version… create a new profile" — see attachment "older version". This is presumably a separate bug, but there's a number of issues here: • it says "an older version of Firefox" instead of Thunderbird • it did not warn me when first running 0228 that I would no longer be able to use the profile from 0227, with Daily 0227. Presumably that profile is now marked as having been used with 0228. • it doesn't offer me the option to "import" my profile from 0227, or any way to correct it. Only to either create a fresh profile, or quit. Does that mean I'm stuffed now until /this/ (empty window) bug is fixed? • isn't this going to make it very difficult for alpha testers — like me — to bisect older builds to find where a problem was introduced, using their existing profiles? I've been doing that for years… If the problem isn't reproducible with an empty profile then it's not going to work. … any suggestions, please? Other than to stop alpha testing Daily as my main email client, that is? :) thanks!

summary: Not all messages in Junk folder marked as read  [was "Mark messages determined to be Junk as read" not working]
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; rv:52.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/52.0 Build ID: 20180621064021 Steps to reproduce: For years I was using an addon called "Mark junk read". What it does is... marks junk as read. All junk, both what thunderbird marks as junk and what the email provider moves into the junk folder. It's a simple and easy task and I really can't believe that thunderbird doesn't have an option for it. Again, I mean ALL junk. I know thunderbird has an option to mark junk as read but it doesn't apply to all junk. Unfortunately with the latest update this addon (as well as most, even some of your featured ones) doesn't work any more. summary: 35 copies of each mail file
bug_report_text: I use Thunderbird with few accounts. One runs Dovecot, two are on GMail. Half of my desktop SSD is used by ~/.thunderbird/ folder nowadays. Decided to take a look why. Went to biggest folder (1.8GB in 57900 files) and then checked how much Thunderbird thinks it takes - 1801 messages, 48.9MB in total. Each message is stored over THIRTY times. Maildir format, GMail account. Went to other folder. 823MB in 77345 files on disk. Thunderbird says 3107 messages, 30.7MB in total. Maildir format again, same GMail account. Ok, let's check Dovecot one. Turns out that this one is mbox based. Biggest one was 2.1GB on disk, 526MB according to Thunderbird. For each of them I went with "repair folder" button which is basically "drop whatever is on disk and fetch again from server". So today instead of working I will go folder by folder to 'repair' them to regain my storage space. And then add to the calendar "go and repair all Thunderbird folders" monthly ;(

summary: Enable PGO builds for Thunderbird
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9122015 PGO-WIP.patch While discussing the difference between a "shippable" build and a regular build as seen in Taskcluster, I mentioned that Firefox's shippable builds use PGO, but Thunderbird's do not. The exact history of why that is I do not know, but it's something to get working since it should be an improvement. A quick initial attempt to bring the configs in from Firefox failed on try. Posting that patch for future reference. Problems: - macOS is disabled, I need to talk to Releng about hardware. - Windows failed because I forgot to add in the Thunderbird version of the tooltool manifest that has the MS MAPI headers - Need to look at the profile-generate job and adjust some paths from "firefox" to "thunderbird"

summary: macOS build with enabled content sandbox crashes on startup, nsMacUtilsImpl::GetRepoDir, GetStringValueFromBundlePlist
bug_report_text: I'm building comm-esr91 on macOS. On startup, I crash here: https://searchfox.org/mozilla-esr91/rev/6de35c9d696f55078a70f4e4ec8dec060e5e93b9/dom/ipc/ContentParent.cpp#2444 The failure happens in GetDirFromBundlePlist, GetStringValueFromBundlePlist, here: https://searchfox.org/mozilla-esr91/rev/6de35c9d696f55078a70f4e4ec8dec060e5e93b9/xpcom/base/nsMacUtilsImpl.cpp#357 I found plugin-container.app/Contents/Info.plist files that contain the <key>MozillaDeveloperRepoPath</key> with a <string>DIR</string> where DIR is an absolute path of the mozilla-esr91 directory. I see the same values in a local build of comm-central, which doesn't crash. I haven't checked if this plist file is the one that is being read.

summary: Check spelling window always opens in upper left corner of screen
bug_report_text: +++ This bug was initially created as a clone of Bug #531833 +++ User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows; U; Windows NT 5.1; en-US; rv:1.9.1.5) Gecko/20091102 Firefox/3.5.5 Build Identifier: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows; U; Windows NT 5.1; en-US; rv:1.8.1.4) Gecko/20070604 Thunderbird/2.0.0.4 Mnenhy/0.7.5.0 After composing a message and clicking send, the spell checking window always opens in the upper left corner of the primary screen regardless of where the message composition window is. Reproducible: Always Steps to Reproduce: 1. Enable "Check spelling before sending" 2. Write a new message 3. Click Send Actual Results: Check Spelling window opens in upper left corner of primary display ... which is so far from the composition window that it's not obvious why the message is not being sent. Expected Results: Check spelling window should open in a position relatively near composition window.

summary: Thunderbird Saved search folders lose criteria - REGULARLY (POP3)
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.3; Win64; x64; rv:61.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/61.0 Build ID: 20180704003137 Steps to reproduce: Setup search folder: - Local folders and all subfolders (589 folders) Criteria: - isnt read - tags isnt 2 - tags isnt 3 - tags isn't 4 Save as search folder. (Windows 8.1, x64, TB Actual results: At first: works fine, 431 results. Then, after a few weeks, results suddenly are about 11 (SOME still show-???) summary: Quick Filter:  Persist user selected text filter button options
bug_report_text:
+++ This bug was initially created as a clone of Bug #1846880 which is a clone of Bug #1833257 +++

**Description**

On application restart, the user's selection for  the buttons in "Filter messages by:" inside Quick Filter settings should be remembered.

*102*
- Current: On restart, user selected button state is retained.
- Desired change: Keep current functionality.

*115*
- Current: On restart, button state is reset to "Sender," "Recipients," and "Subject."
- Desired change: On restart, remember the user’s selection and set the buttons to that state.

This returns functionality in 115 to what is the current functionality in 102.

When switching folders, user selected button state is retained. (Working as expected in 102 and 115)

----------

Original report from Thomas D. :thomas8) from bug 18233257 comment #0)
Seen on 115.0a1 (2023-05-15) (64-bit), Win10.

> From post on TB-Beta, [Can the "keep filters applied" toggle be made sticky?](https://thunderbird.topicbox.com/groups/beta/T4197af010185c29c-Md06476e00aecc98e120e52c8)

> TB 102 used to persist the status of quick filter buttons across TB restart, but Daily doesn't do that any more.

> STR
> - Switch on `Keep filters applied when changing folders` and `Unread` quick filter.
> - Restart TB
> - Observe status of the filter buttons

> Actual
> - filter button status not persisted

> Expected
> - Should probably just persist filter button status as in 102. 
> - Otherwise persisting filter states should probably be available as an option.
> - It's worth noting that the filter *text* is *not* persisted, probably by design.
> - The rationale here is that the buttons act like view filters, which users may want to have applied always depending on their workflows. Which is different from filter text, which targets specific messages.

(Working as expected in 102 and 115) ---------- Original report from Thomas D. :thomas8) from bug 18233257 comment #0) Seen on 115.0a1 (2023-05-15) (64-bit), Win10. > From post on TB-Beta, [Can the "keep filters applied" toggle be made sticky?](https://thunderbird.topicbox.com/groups/beta/T4197af010185c29c-Md06476e00aecc98e120e52c8) > TB 102 used to persist the status of quick filter buttons across TB restart, but Daily doesn't do that any more. > STR > - Switch on `Keep filters applied when changing folders` and `Unread` quick filter. > - Restart TB > - Observe status of the filter buttons > Actual > - filter button status not persisted > Expected > - Should probably just persist filter button status as in 102. > - Otherwise persisting filter states should probably be available as an option. > - It's worth noting that the filter *text* is *not* persisted, probably by design. > - The rationale here is that the buttons act like view filters, which users may want to have applied always depending on their workflows. Which is different from filter text, which targets specific messages.

summary: Forward as Attachments freezes Thunderbird for a few minutes with high memory when used on 201 emails
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:73.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/73.0 Steps to reproduce: 1. Press ctrl+A in folder with a very large number of emails 2. Right click 3. Select the top option "Forward as Attachments" I triggered this bug accidentally because the item is the top one in the right-click menu. In my case the folder had 201 emails. Actual results: Thunderbird completely freezes for several minutes using up several gigabytes of RAM. Once it finishes an ordinary new email dialog with a lot of attachments is opened, and I can quit it normally. The RAM usage went down again when the new email dialog opened. In my case there were 201 emails in the folder. Expected results: An error dialog or loading bar should have shown up, or perhaps it plain shouldn't have taken very long. It seems like it is doing something very inefficient in the process of creating the attachments, because it used several gigabytes of RAM to create 11 MB of attachments from 201 emails.

summary: After upgrading to 66.0b build 3, profile manager repeatedly asks to select a profile on startup
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; Win64; x64; rv:60.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/60.0 SeaMonkey/2.57 Steps to reproduce: I upgraded to TB 66.0b1 Build 3 from 65.0b4 Build 2. Actual results: Upon app launch, the profile manager asks which profile to use. It kept asking every time I launched the app until I chose the Default profile and checked the box for "Do not ask again." Expected results: Upon app launch, the app should just start as normal and not ask which profile to use.

summary: Bs7 jobs failing on nightly Thunderbird builds
bug_report_text: After https://hg.mozilla.org/comm-central/rev/3f02fbbeaffa landed, the Bs7 job started failing on Thunderbird dailies. This is fallout from changes done to accommodate empty signing formats for notarization-part-3. Previously, after removing all of the unused signing formats, if the signing format list was empty, the artifacts were completely removed from the signing job. But now when the build-signing job has notarization dependencies, an empty format list is ignored. But that ja-JP-mac locale has exception code somewhere that makes it stick around. ``` "upstreamArtifacts": [ { "formats": [], "paths": [ "public/build/hy-AM/target.pkg", "public/build/hy-AM/target.tar.gz", "public/build/id/target.pkg", "public/build/id/target.tar.gz", "public/build/is/target.pkg", "public/build/is/target.tar.gz", "public/build/it/target.pkg", "public/build/it/target.tar.gz", "public/build/ja-JP-mac/target.pkg", "public/build/ja-JP-mac/target.tar.gz" ], "taskId": "IV2gGuDASxqHYyuxTq9LNw", "taskType": "scriptworker" }, { "formats": [], "paths": [ "public/build/ja-JP-mac/target.langpack.xpi" ], "taskId": "IV2gGuDASxqHYyuxTq9LNw", "taskType": "scriptworker" } ] ```

summary: Master Password requester appears twice on startup, one is superfluous?
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:63.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/63.0 Steps to reproduce: 60.3.2 32bit. W7 Hm Prem. Tbird working with 1 account direct and 4 imap accounts. Master Password only requested once on builds prior to this, after Add-on not needed anymore due to innate Tbird improvements. Now this behaviour has changed. Actual results: On opening the program, a Master Password box immediately comes up, then is covered by the main program window and another Master Password box comes up. You can enter the password in the 2nd box and it all seems to work fine. The covered box remains and appears to be able to be simply cancelled. This is new and only appeared in this build. Expected results: Only one Master Password box should come up.

summary: Setting up mozilla, comm, and mach build for Thunderbird 64.0
bug_report_text: Up to Thunderbird 61.0, I could build TB from within comm-release with a sub-directory mozilla by running './mozilla/mach build'. comm-release and mozilla directories contain clones of the mercurial repositories comm-release and mozilla-unified. I haven't tried to build TB 62.x or 63.x, but have now tried to build TB 64.0. That build setup no longer works, getting errors like: ERROR: Cannot find project mail which actually means: ERROR: Cannot find /path/to/comm-release/mozilla/mail/moz.configure .. meaning that the build is looking in mozilla for the mail directory which is in comm-release. The problem seems to be that topsrcdir is set as mozilla, which works fine for Firefox, but there's no allowance made for the TB source which is in a different directory. I've tried setting EXTERNAL_SOURCE_DIR=/path/to/comm-release but that didn't help. Also tried setting comm as a sub-directory of mozilla, and building from comm and from mozilla but still get the build looking in the wrong place - /path/to/mozilla/mail/moz.configure, which should be /path/to/mozilla/comm/mail/moz.configure. I managed a successful build using work-arounds, and the result runs ok: [1] by setting 'comm-release' as a directory 'comm' within 'mozilla' [2] linking 'mail*' from 'comm' into 'mozilla' to avoid the above path error [3] in /path/to/mozilla/comm/mail/moz.configure, replacing include('../../toolkit/moz.configure') with include('../toolkit/moz.configure') I've found some references that the build method is moving to [1], but can't find any detail on how to set up the build to access the {comm/mail*,toolkit}/moz.configure files. But I'm sure that [2] and [3] aren't the way to go. What needs to be set so that the moz.configure files are found?

summary: Thunderbird 68.5.0esr doesn't open URIs
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.3; rv:68.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/68.0 Steps to reproduce: 1) Open Thenderbird 68.5.0esr 2) Kill explorer.exe process in Task Manager 3) Open an e-mail containing links (for example to imgur.com) 4) Click the blue link Actual results: 5) The link is violet but no action is done. In Console: ```NS_ERROR_FAILURE: Component returned failure code: 0x80004005 (NS_ERROR_FAILURE) [nsIExternalProtocolService.loadURI] contentAreaClick.js:230 openLinkExternally chrome://communicator/content/contentAreaClick.js:230 contentAreaClick chrome://communicator/content/contentAreaClick.js:200 onclick chrome://messenger/content/messenger.xul:1``` Expected results: 5) if browser is not run, the browser should be run. 6) new tab with imgur.com should be visible. My company uses windows shell replacement. The minimal test is killing the explorer.exe and trying to open a link. Last correctly working version is 68.2.0esr. We must migrate to other mail-client due to not corrected bug (not working basic functionality) and new security issues from 68.3.0 to 68.5.0. Changes starting from 68.3.0 (not working attachments) and 68.3.1 (not working URIs) is any misunderstanding. Additionally: if any other application will replace used COM+ objects it is possible to elevate privileges (security issue) because third party application can handle COM+ object instead of explorer.exe. There is needed only second registration of these objects and optionally force restart explorer.exe for example by sending WM_CLOSE or WM_DESTROY to shell windows. This behaviour is strongly leaky, additionally it won't be corrected in Windows 7 (end of support).

bug_report_text_clean: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.3; rv:68.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/68.0 Steps to reproduce: 1) Open Thenderbird 68.5.0esr 2) Kill explorer.exe process in Task Manager 3) Open an e-mail containing links (for example to imgur.com) 4) Click the blue link Actual results: 5) The link is violet but no action is done. In Console: ```NS_ERROR_FAILURE: Component returned failure code: 0x80004005 (NS_ERROR_FAILURE) [nsIExternalProtocolService.loadURI] contentAreaClick.js:230 openLinkExternally chrome://communicator/content/contentAreaClick.js:230 contentAreaClick chrome://communicator/content/contentAreaClick.js:200 onclick chrome://messenger/content/messenger.xul:1``` Expected results: 5) if browser is not run, the browser should be run. 6) new tab with imgur.com should be visible. My company uses windows shell replacement. The minimal test is killing the explorer.exe and trying to open a link. Last correctly working version is 68.2.0esr. We must migrate to other mail-client due to not corrected bug (not working basic functionality) and new security issues from 68.3.0 to 68.5.0. Changes starting from 68.3.0 (not working attachments) and 68.3.1 (not working URIs) is any misunderstanding. Additionally: if any other application will replace used COM+ objects it is possible to elevate privileges (security issue) because third party application can handle COM+ object instead of explorer.exe. There is needed only second registration of these objects and optionally force restart explorer.exe for example by sending WM_CLOSE or WM_DESTROY to shell windows. This behaviour is strongly leaky, additionally it won't be corrected in Windows 7 (end of support).

summary: frequent short freeze of 3-5 seconds under Windows 10 and Unresponsive Script (while getting mail?)
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/74.0.3729.157 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: I am just using Thunderbird and I get regulary short freezes or unresponsible application messages. Actual results: I am having this issue with temporarily freezing Thunderbird 60.6.1 under Windows 10 1809, too. Also in previous versions of Windows 10 and Thunderbird. I have deavtivated Windows Search. I am using 10 e-mails accounts with TB. Three POP3S and seven IMAPS. The freezing is happening radomly, while writing, while searching, while reading. I also stopped indexing of hard disk C:, but it still freezes. I have already removed all Add-Ons and tried it in safe mode. No success. Any ideas what I can do with this strange behaviour? Expected results: No freeze at all.

summary: False positive from PhishingDetector.jsm for encoded URL (same as link text) with pointless alert : 'The link you just clicked seems to lead to another site... link text... 49.12.10.46, but it leads to 49.12.10.46'
bug_report_text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:102.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/102.0

Steps to reproduce:

Click a link in received (from reliable source) eMail 


Actual results:

Strange message:
The link you just clicked seems to lead to another site than what the link text indicated. This is sometimes used for tracking whether you clicked the link, but it could also be a scam.

The link text indicated that the link would lead to 49.12.10.46, but it leads to 49.12.10.46.

With buttons:  Go to 49.12.10.46 anyway; Cancel; Go to 49.12.10.46


Expected results:

No such message for same IP 

The link is:
http://49.12.10.46/debug_fb50/windows/fbt_show_cross_report.has-some-errors.htm#o13513.16%23

The text is:
http://49.12.10.46/debug_fb50/windows/fbt_show_cross_report.has-some-errors.htm#o13513.16#

Suppose that may happen due to # vs %23 but error text is a little funny :)

summary: "Not Responding"  1-2 minutes when moving Outlook/Live.com IMAP folder since update to 52.9.1
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/65.0.3325.183 Safari/537.36 Vivaldi/1.96.1147.55 Steps to reproduce: When I either drag and drop emails from one folder to another or right-click and use the drop-down menu to move them. Actual results: I get (Not Responding) anywhere from 30 seconds to 2 minutes. It seems to Not Respond for a longer period of time when I drag and drop, rather than when I use the drop-down menu although even it can still take up to a minute. This has been happening since the last update: 52.9.1. It is only happening with my Outlook accounts. My Gmail accounts are still working fine. I would have specified a version but did not see this one in the drop-down list above. Expected results: Files should move to selected folder without delay.
text: "Not Responding" 1-2 minutes when moving Outlook/Live.com IMAP folder since update to 52.9.1 [SEP] User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/65.0.3325.183 Safari/537.36 Vivaldi/1.96.1147.55 Steps to reproduce: When I either drag and drop emails from one folder to another or right-click and use the drop-down menu to move them. Actual results: I get (Not Responding) anywhere from 30 seconds to 2 minutes. It seems to Not Respond for a longer period of time when I drag and drop, rather than when I use the drop-down menu although even it can still take up to a minute. This has been happening since the last update: 52.9.1. It is only happening with my Outlook accounts. My Gmail accounts are still working fine. I would have specified a version but did not see this one in the drop-down list above. Expected results: Files should move to selected folder without delay.

summary: Thunderbird 98.0a1 (2022-02-07) and 98.0b1/b2 Crash in [@ js::RunScript ] or [@ PromiseReactionJob ] after closing/shutdown
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Fedora; Linux x86_64; rv:96.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/96.0 Steps to reproduce: I updated Thunderbird 98.0a1 on Wayland from 2022-02-06 to 2022-02-07 using the Help > About Daily window in a Fedora 35 KDE Plasma installation with Plasma 5.23.5 on Wayland. I started Thunderbird 98.0a1 (2022-02-07) and used it. I closed Thunderbird. Actual results: After I closed Thunderbird 98.0a1 (2022-02-07), I tried to start it again within 30 seconds. An error box had the message "Thunderbird is already running, but is not responding. To use Thunderbird, you must first close the existing Thunderbird process, restart your device, or use a different profile." The Thunderbird Crash Reporter appeared about a minute after closing Thunderbird. The first crash report had the signature [@ js::RunScript ] and was in NS_DebugBreak at hg:hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-unified:xpcom/base/nsDebugImpl.cpp:9212c99a00f195539b334c8cfea48786448969b0:433 The crash reason was [Parent 1986, Main Thread] ###!!! ABORT: file resource://gre/modules/CrashMonitor.jsm:175 https://crash-stats.thunderbird.net/report/bp-eda32b84-a509-4a57-835a-2f77e1220207#tab-details Thunderbird 98.0a1 (2022-02-07) crashed 8/8 times after closing, 6 of which had the signature [@ PromiseReactionJob ] with what looked to be the same trace and reason as above. https://crash-stats.thunderbird.net/report/bp-89e36cf0-e3b6-471c-a70f-c8f411220207 I tried to use mozregression to bisect the problem, but it didn't download anything to test. mozregression --app thunderbird --good 2022-02-06 --bad 2022-02-07 0:01.19 INFO: Got as far as we can go bisecting nightlies... 0:01.19 INFO: Last good revision: a4c122858ebe9bb672b411e093e6342a53a1243a (2022-02-06) 0:01.19 INFO: First bad revision: 1869b10836585e6f7e9b510c1f96f389b6392936 (2022-02-07) 0:01.19 INFO: Pushlog: https://hg.mozilla.org/comm-central/pushloghtml?fromchange=a4c122858ebe9bb672b411e093e6342a53a1243a&tochange=1869b10836585e6f7e9b510c1f96f389b6392936 0:01.19 INFO: Switching bisection method to taskcluster 0:01.19 INFO: Getting comm-central builds between a4c122858ebe9bb672b411e093e6342a53a1243a and 1869b10836585e6f7e9b510c1f96f389b6392936 0:02.47 INFO: No more integration revisions, bisection finished. 0:02.47 INFO: Last good revision: a4c122858ebe9bb672b411e093e6342a53a1243a 0:02.47 INFO: First bad revision: 1869b10836585e6f7e9b510c1f96f389b6392936 0:02.47 INFO: Pushlog: https://hg.mozilla.org/comm-central/pushloghtml?fromchange=a4c122858ebe9bb672b411e093e6342a53a1243a&tochange=1869b10836585e6f7e9b510c1f96f389b6392936 Expected results: Thunderbird would close normally.

I've been pushed to Upgrade to Version 66.0b3, I think, although it keeps trying to tell me to manually D.L a FireFox Upgrade, because the Automatic one, can't finish properly? But it's done the same thing, after I D/Led, & installed several, so guess that didn't FIX anything yet? But Additionally, this new Ver. 66.xxx Thunder Bird Doesn't allow me to Delete a Message, once I'm viewing it on my Screen? So, I found the Article Where it says I should find the Trash Folder, & delete it, as this is the IMAP type/Version of T Bird? Only this T Bird appears to to have at least 2 Trash & Trash.msi Folders? Well I Deleted both of them, Serially, Restarted, & it hasn't seemed to FIX that Problem either? Don't know what to do to FIX either of these problems, or Where to go from here either?? If you are the wrong Grue to send this too, could you at least pass it on to someone, who can HELP ME, Por Favor!! Paul Edwards XXXXXXXXXXXXX (Cell., But I'm almost totally Deaf, so my wife will have to Translate?) Stevensville, Montana Actual results: Nothing that I am aware of? Expected results: Problem would be Fixed?

summary: emails are respawning from deletion
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9017855 tbird_log.txt User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.11; rv:62.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/62.0 Steps to reproduce: Hello I use TB with orange imap servers I only have one imap account set on this adress (no other devices at all) I have 20000 emails in trash Actual results: I do have 20000 emails lying in my trash folder. they are respawning from deletion. tested by deleting all them from TB interface, waited for sync, got some server timeout but the process ends with success. I then went to the webmail, look for TRASH folder is empty as expected. Quit TB, then use it a couple of days, and I get back all my deleted mails in TRASH I tried to add some extra timeout delay in advanced prefs to no avail. The internet connection is steady but the orange servers may overload, it looks like the IMAP communication fails at some point Expected results: Those emails should be gone, or I would get an error message from TB Installed Outlook 2016 and the behavior is just fine on the same provider/account

summary: Thunderbird ignores or saves  SLEEP command on OSX 10.14.6
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_14_6) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/12.1.2 Safari/605.1.15 Steps to reproduce: select Apple Menu-Sleep command 2018 MacBook Pro OSX 10.14.6 started noticing it on V73b versions REPLY or notify steve@jasik.com Actual results: NOTHING - Expected results: machine should go to sleep (screen turns black) NOTE: It started happening with V73 beta so I updated to V75.2 and it seemed to go away for a day, but it returned. If TBird is not active, then the Sleep command works find. If I select Sleep with TB active nothing happens until I quit TB and then the screen turns off.

summary: Crash in [@ mozilla::detail::nsTStringRepr<T>::Last | nsImapIncomingServer::FEAlertFromServer]
bug_report_text: On Jan 19, in a few short hours, we see a large spike in crashes, suspiciously around midnight UTC, as seen in this [48 hour graph](https://crash-stats.mozilla.org/signature/?product=Thunderbird&version=102.6.1&signature=mozilla%3A%3Adetail%3A%3AnsTStringRepr%3CT%3E%3A%3ALast%20%7C%20nsImapIncomingServer%3A%3AFEAlertFromServer&date=%3E%3D2023-01-18T13%3A23%3A00.000Z&date=%3C2023-01-20T16%3A23%3A00.000Z&_columns=date&_columns=product&_columns=version&_columns=build_id&_columns=platform&_columns=reason&_columns=address&_columns=install_time&_columns=startup_crash&_sort=&page=4#graphs). Ranks #37 for the week, but for 102.6.0 there is no such spike, ranks #117 for the week. Spot checked and was not able to find other crash signatures with such a spike. This spike comes long after 102.6.1 was released in December. I'm not concerned about the spike, which is gone. But whether there is some underlying condition. Crash report: https://crash-stats.mozilla.org/report/index/1d27b190-40c8-4555-9a1c-618a80230120. Windows 10 MOZ_CRASH Reason: ```MOZ_RELEASE_ASSERT(this->mLength > 0) (|Last()| called on an empty string)``` Top 10 frames of crashing thread: ``` 0 xul.dll mozilla::detail::nsTStringRepr<char>::Last const xpcom/string/nsTSubstring.cpp:962 1 xul.dll nsImapIncomingServer::FEAlertFromServer mailnews/imap/src/nsImapIncomingServer.cpp:1767 2 xul.dll `anonymous namespace'::SyncRunnable2<nsIImapServerSink, const nsTSubstring<char16_t>&, nsIMsgMailNewsUrl*>::Run mailnews/imap/src/nsSyncRunnableHelpers.cpp:121 3 xul.dll mozilla::RunnableTask::Run xpcom/threads/TaskController.cpp:475 4 xul.dll mozilla::TaskController::DoExecuteNextTaskOnlyMainThreadInternal xpcom/threads/TaskController.cpp:788 5 xul.dll mozilla::TaskController::ExecuteNextTaskOnlyMainThreadInternal xpcom/threads/TaskController.cpp:620 6 xul.dll mozilla::TaskController::ProcessPendingMTTask xpcom/threads/TaskController.cpp:398 7 xul.dll mozilla::detail::RunnableFunction<`lambda at /builds/worker/checkouts/gecko/xpcom/threads/TaskController.cpp:124:7'>::Run xpcom/threads/nsThreadUtils.h:531 8 xul.dll nsThread::ProcessNextEvent xpcom/threads/nsThread.cpp:1180 9 xul.dll NS_ProcessNextEvent xpcom/threads/nsThreadUtils.cpp:465 ``` bp-a33653f1-29bd-4b07-aeb7-d0c4a0230119 Mac

summary: Downgrading from 60.3.0 to 52.9.1 deletes saved login data and master password
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; Win64; x64; rv:56.0; Waterfox) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/56.2.5 Steps to reproduce: Upgraded from 52.9.1 to 60.3.0 in-app. After realizing that this makes half of my addons unusable, I downgraded manually (installed 52.9.1 over 60.3.0). Actual results: All saved login data (usernames, passwords) and the master password itself were lost. Not even a warning was given. Expected results: Login data and the master password should have been preserved.

summary: 78.4 for OSX not Responding and high memory after update installed
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_14_6) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/86.0.4240.111 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: I updated to version 78.4 last night (11/3) per alert on my Macbook Pro - running 10.14.6, 8 GB memory, I7. Actual results: Immediately on launch it started gobbling up memory and stopped responding (beach ball) - so I have had to force quit to get out. After 15 years I have never seen anything like this, and I can't live with it very long. Should I go to MacMail and abandon T-bird? Expected results: Just routine email retrieval and composition, like always.

summary: Connections refused POP IMAP Newsgroup servers. Broken Handshake/ TLS?
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/64.0.3282.140 Safari/537.36 Edge/17.17134 Steps to reproduce: Unable to connect to imap.gmail & pop.verizon & news.gname. Connections stopped 11/23. Waited for v60.3.2 , no luck. With Torbirdy enabled connections are refused. I don't care for TOR. With torbirdy diabled : Sending of password for user *@verizon.net did not succeed. Mail server pop.verizon.net responded: [SYS/TEMP] Server error - Please try again later. THIS happened briefly: Sending of password for user *@verizon.net did not succeed. Mail server pop.verizon.net responded: UGFzc3dvcmQ6 Login to server imap.gmail.com with username *@gmail.com failed. summary: [Mac] When updating from 68.12.0 to 78.2.1, thunderbird hangs high memory about 2 seconds after startup while reading from the Mac system address-book
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_6) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/13.1.2 Safari/605.1.15 Steps to reproduce: Update from 68.12.0 to 78.2.1 as the gpg integration is now productive. After installing the update I started Thunderbird. Actual results: Shortly after the start (enough time to launch one action like opening the adressbook via a shortcut) Thunderbird hangs and doesn't react to any more input. From observing it it slowly starts to gobble up more RAM - I have waited until it used about 150GB RAM, then I had to kill it as my system was becoming unresponsive. (This took probably about half an hour). Observing its stack traces during that time it looked as if Thunderbird was reading from the system adressbook with pretty much all threads. I was able to reproduce this behaviour reliably. Expected results: Thunderbird should just start, and not hang reading the system adressbook. Not sure what kind of accidental exponential behaviour it has while reading addresses, but that should be fixed. If I can help by providing additional debugging information - I'd like to help.

summary: Thunderbird crashes when particular command line arguments are passed
bug_report_text: Steps to reproduce: - Set Thunderbird as the handler for mailto: URLs - Click on a mailto: URL in Firefox Thunderbird (ESR) is installed via the Microsoft Store. I have only a single extension installed (Birthday Calendar). Actual results: Apparently nothing. The event viewer shows an Application Error and a Windows Error Reporting message: P1: MozillaThunderbird.MZLA_140.7.1.0_x64__h5892qc0xkpca P2: praid:App P3: 140.7.1.0 P4: 6973ebcc P5: thunderbird.exe P6: 140.7.1.0 P7: 6973ebcc P8: c0000005 P9: 0000000000001ebc P10: Expected results: Thunderbird should not crash, but open the mail compose window.

summary: From CC & Bcc & >> buttons are going out of the window if From field text is too long
bug_report_text:
Steps to reproduce:

1. Write a new message
2. Select a Sender's identity with a long text
3. Resize the window



Actual results:

After step 2 : From field resize and push Bcc and >> buttons out of the windows' UI
After step 3: no change


Expected results:

From field text should be truncated with '...' (like in TB 102).

Regression : works in TB 102.7.1

summary: Slow to quit, idle with responsive UI for several seconds on shutdown. Workaround, go "offline mode" before quitting
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:106.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/106.0 Steps to reproduce: Thunderbird is slow to quit; started with 105 beta 1. Issue persisted all through to current 106 beta 3. I'm on Linux64 release from tar. Snap beta release exhibits same behavior for me (with same profile data). The only thing I've found, is that going into "offline mode" before quitting fixes this -- quit is instant then. Actual results: Ctrl+q OR "Quit" from menu OR via "X" ui element results in Thunderbird idling for a few seconds while UI remains responsive. Sometimes the message list disappears. But it'll eventually shut down clean. Expected results: Quit when prompted to.

bug_report_text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:103.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/103.0 Steps to reproduce: Continued to use Thunderbird after the (automatic through the Gnome Software application manager) upgrade to 102.0.2 No change on my part from the way i have used Thunderbird from the past month, year, decade. Actual results: On Sunday, i did not see an Yesterday, there were conspicuously no messages received after 12:32pm. To quote myself giving the heads up to my coworkers: > seems about a day into using the new Thunderbird it is no longer getting e-mail. If i press "Get Messages" it does nothing. If i tell it to get a specific accounts e-mail, it tells me to wait for it to be done processing. Oh, now it says host contacted, sending login information… but it must be failing silently in the background, maybe some certificate or password it needs that it is not asking me for. Unless May First e-mail is down. > > And the search is still picking a handful of results out of the much larger amount it should give, but that seemed to start a few days before the update. > > is my fear that they focused on the UI and broke everything come true? > > or can it just not handle several hundred thousand e-mails? I don't think i'm even at a million, > > Disabled google-connected calendars because why not, they were always slow and they are even slower in the new TBird 102 > > 23rd try to get e-mail just for the main Agaric account and it claims to be "Downloading message 2 of 37" but it's been saying that for a while now. E-mail is fetched from POP without problem and sorted into local folders according to the rules set up. E-mail messages that have just been downloaded are indicated in blue. It is easy to see what has been downloaded, they are seen immediately, and we have confidence messages are not being lost into oblivion. I am on Debian testing. If this same experience is rolled out to other longtime users, say who are likely getting it from apt in Ubuntu, it is going to be pretty devastating to the community. I am a financial supporter of Thunderbird and many others likely to be affected are also. PLEASE test these upgrades against some 20-year-old profiles with decades of mail! Disruptive changes should have a guided migration. What is happening right now to the place where i have two decades of e-mail (a few years of that imported from Opera Mail) is SCARY. Please help. Thank you.

summary: Superb 'Show InOut' does not work in 68
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:74.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/74.0 Steps to reproduce: I've used Thunderbird for years up to version 60 using Show InOut and XNote. I was forcibly regraded to 68 recently. I eventually got XNote to work but I am still stuck with separate Inbox and Sent folders. Actual results: Tried everything I can think of without hoped for result. I tried to regrade back to version 60 from my backups but this was immediately forcibly regraded back to 68 when I loaded it. Expected results: In version 60 InOut displayed the Inbox and Sent mails all in the Inbox folder. The In version 60 Show Inout displayed the Inbox mails and the Sent mails both in the Inbox folder. The Inbox mails' texts were displayed in italics (I nbox - I talics - very logical !). The In mails and Sent mails were all correctly threaded. In 68 the Inbox mails are still correctly threaded but the separate Inbox and Sent folders are a definite, definite downgrade. Unfortunately, since I cannot run 60 any more I cannot attach a Snipping JPEG file of its Inbox.
text: Superb 'Show InOut' does not work in 68 [SEP] User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:74.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/74.0 Steps to reproduce: I've used Thunderbird for years up to version 60 using Show InOut and XNote. I was forcibly regraded to 68 recently. I eventually got XNote to work but I am still stuck with separate Inbox and Sent folders. Actual results: Tried everything I can think of without hoped for result. I tried to regrade back to version 60 from my backups but this was immediately forcibly regraded back to 68 when I loaded it. Expected results: In version 60 InOut displayed the Inbox and Sent mails all in the Inbox folder. The In version 60 Show Inout displayed the Inbox mails and the Sent mails both in the Inbox folder. The Inbox mails' texts were displayed in italics (I nbox - I talics - very logical !). The In mails and Sent mails were all correctly threaded. In 68 the Inbox mails are still correctly threaded but the separate Inbox and Sent folders are a definite, definite downgrade. Unfortunately, since I cannot run 60 any more I cannot attach a Snipping JPEG file of its Inbox.

summary: Recently created tags vanish.
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/74.0.3729.169 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: I have several tags. Those I created some time ago - a few weeks - work properly. Those I have created in the last few weeks disappear after a while. This only happens in the Inbox. All tags in all other mailboxes work properly. I have tried safe mode and even an older version of TB (but still with the same profile). Clicking away from the Inbox then back into it will cause the tags to vanish. But only the newer ones.

summary: Corrupt msf file causes Thunderbird to hang exactly 5 minutes after startup (purge service)
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/146.0.0.0 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Thunderbird 149.0.2 (build 20260403233102) freezes at exactly 5 minutes after startup every session, regardless of activity. CPU jumps from ~1% to 18-20% at the freeze point and the application stops responding. Fresh install of 149. Troubleshooting performed: deleted and disabled the Gloda global search index (mailnews.database.global.indexer.enabled = false); ran in Troubleshoot Mode with all extensions disabled; ran in offline mode (File → Offline → Work Offline); added Thunderbird to Windows Defender exclusions; reduced mailnews.tcptimeout from 600 to 29; set mailnews.autosync_timer_interval to 45 seconds; disabled mail.server.server4.autosync_offline_stores; disabled network.connectivity-service.enabled, network.captive-portal-service.enabled, security.OCSP.enabled, services.settings.poll_interval, and app.update.enabled. Actual results: None of these changes affected the freeze timing or behavior. Windows Resource Monitor Wait Chain analysis shows the frozen process as "waiting to finish network I/O," but Network tab confirms the only active connections are two IPv4 loopback connections (IPC between Thunderbird's own processes) — no external network connections exist at the time of freeze. This indicates the hang is caused by an internal IPC timeout between Thunderbird's parent and child processes, triggered by a background routine firing at exactly T+300 seconds after startup. Profile has been in use since 2019 and ran without issue on prior Thunderbird versions. Expected results: This behavior started a couple weeks ago. I was configuring some new email addresses, so my first impression was something in the profile. I've since removed all the new account settings, restored to a profile I've used for many years, and then took the additional troubleshooting steps above. In all cases (online and offline), Thunderbird opens and runs normally for 5 minutes, then hangs.

summary: The preferences dialog stucks on Display → Tags tab
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:52.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/52.0 SeaMonkey/2.49.4 Build ID: 20180711183816 Steps to reproduce: Probably this is caused by a large number of tags (they were gathered automatically from some RSS feeds). The tab is remembered and opened by default every time the preferences dialog is opened. If I restart TB in "Add-ons disabled" mode, the issue is gone, but only until I visit that tab again. Actual results: When the tab is opened, the dialog hangs for a while, then stucks here. I can switch second-level tabs, either by clicking or by Ctrl+Tab, but clicking on top-level tabs does nothing, as well as clicking on controls in other second-level tabs (Formatting, Advanced). If applicable, there are messages falling to console: Error: A long running script was terminated menulist.xml:136:1 Error: Script terminated by timeout at: get_editable@chrome://global/content/bindings/menulist.xml:136:1 _selectPane@chrome://messenger/content/preferences/preferences.xml:826:19 observe@chrome://messenger/content/preferences/preferences.xml:756:17 menulist.xml:136:1 NS_ERROR_NOT_AVAILABLE: Cannot call openModalWindow on a hidden window nsPrompter.js:340 TypeError: Argument 1 of Window.getComputedStyle is not an object.[Learn More] preferences.xml:1296:39 Expected results: The tab doesn't prevent going to other top-level tabs.

summary: Thunderbird freeze periodically after Dezember Update 85.0b2
bug_report_text: User Agent: null Steps to reproduce: After i updated my stable thunderbird release in mid Dezember, it now freeze periodacally. No respond in main window, calender, mail composer etc. Actual results: The window does not turn white and windows os does not say it would not respond, but inside of thunderbird nothing happens. If i wait an undefinied time of minutes it works again until its not work and so on. If i restart Thunderbird it works right away but freezes after time x. Upgrade to 85b2 did not resolved the problem. Hardware acceleration deactivation did not resolved the problem remove addons did not resolve the problem CPU is running vom 0.9% to 5% but no high load. HDD usage on startup goes up to 40mb/s but after that it declines. Windows 10 no changes in antivirus software. Expected results: Thunderbird responds every time.

Actual results: Update notification bar. Auto Restarted. .All data was lost. Needed to Re-Login and Re-set up to my email address. ... Inbox recovered only. All added Local Folders have gone causing me to lose links to House/ Bike/ Car Insurance, Gas Safe Certificates, Holiday Information, etc Expected results: Auto Re-Start with no issues.

bug_report_text: #7 crash for Macs for 68.5.0. In the past 6 months there are not crashes prior to 68.3.0, so regression? Further, the earliest non-release crash is 71.0b1? buildid 20191113091023 bp-f4eae0cb-736f-43a5-af3e-b1e720191128 https://crash-stats.mozilla.com/signature/?product=Thunderbird&signature=ldap_abandon_ext&date=%3E%3D2019-08-25T03%3A02%3A00.000Z&date=%3C2020-02-25T03%3A02%3A00.000Z#graphs This bug is for crash report bp-a72338e5-8aaa-456e-8ba9-4fa130200225. 75.0a1 ``` Top 10 frames of crashing thread: 0 libldap60.dylib ldap_abandon_ext comm/ldap/c-sdk/libraries/libldap/abandon.c:96 1 XUL XUL@0x96f0 2 XUL XUL@0x447d9e 3 XUL XUL@0x44b558 4 XUL XUL@0x54ced5 5 XUL XUL@0x54dc5c 6 XUL XUL@0x447d9e 7 XUL XUL@0x44b558 8 XUL XUL@0xa07cf3 9 XUL XUL@0x9c0655 ``` This happened on shutdown, after I had attempted to delete the Mac OSX Address Book, which resulted in the following in the Error Console. NS_ERROR_UNEXPECTED: Unexpected error'Unexpected error' when calling method: [nsIAbManager::deleteAddressBook] abCommon.js:372 21:49:42.542 An error occurred executing the button_delete command: [Exception... "Unexpected error'Unexpected error' when calling method: [nsIAbManager::deleteAddressBook]" nsresult: "0x8000ffff (NS_ERROR_UNEXPECTED)" location: "JS frame :: chrome://messenger/content/addressbook/abCommon.js :: AbDeleteDirectory :: line 372" data: no] globalOverlay.js:104 Whether this triggered the crash remains to be seen. I'll try to reproduce. For good measure - bp-ae11c080-2fc2-4014-b4b5-5fcdb0200225 68.5.0

summary: 60.6.1 (32-bit) - Software update failed on start-up
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:66.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/66.0 Steps to reproduce: Installed fresh copy of Thunderbird on Monday, 25.03.2019. Today, 29.03.2019 when I started the program pop-up appeard with the title Software update failed and the message The update could not be installed. Please make sure there are no other copies of Thunderbird running on your computer, and then restart Thunderbird to try again. Going to Help>About Mozilla Thunderbird and clicking restart to update did nothing. Same error when Thunderbird starts. Killing Thunderbird process from Task Manager did not help. Same result on Thunderbird start. Downloaded the 60.6.1 version and installed it over the existing one but the error keeps appearing. Actual results: Error box Software update failed and the message The update could not be installed. Please make sure there are no other copies of Thunderbird running on your computer, and then restart Thunderbird to try again keep appearing every time Thunderbird starts. Expected results: Update the version and work without errors.

summary: Automatic update does not replace MAPI DLLs
bug_report_text: We had quite some drama when shipping changes to MAPI, see bug 1530820 with its bazillion duplicates. Turn out that the automated update does not fully replace the MAPI DLLs. Before the update: 60.5.1 DLLs: C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\MapiProxy_InUse.dll - 22 KB - 2019/02/13 C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\mozMapi32_InUse.dll - 97 KB - 2019/02/13 Identical files MapiProxy.dll and mozMapi32.dll After update to 60.5.2, these are still there: C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\MapiProxy_InUse.dll - 22 KB - 2019/02/13 C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\mozMapi32_InUse.dll - 97 KB - 2019/02/13 And also: C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\mozMapi32.dll - 96 KB - 2019/03/01 C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\MapiProxy.dll - 22 KB - 2019/03/01 2019/03/01 is the date of the auto-update. As we can see, the mozMapi32.dll has changed, but the "InUse" version wasn't updated :-( Nice mess. Richard, can you please take a look and ask someone in M-C?

Before the update: 60.5.1 DLLs: C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\MapiProxy_InUse.dll - 22 KB - 2019/02/13 C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\mozMapi32_InUse.dll - 97 KB - 2019/02/13 Identical files MapiProxy.dll and mozMapi32.dll After update to 60.5.2, these are still there: C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\MapiProxy_InUse.dll - 22 KB - 2019/02/13 C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\mozMapi32_InUse.dll - 97 KB - 2019/02/13 And also: C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\mozMapi32.dll - 96 KB - 2019/03/01 C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird 60\MapiProxy.dll - 22 KB - 2019/03/01 2019/03/01 is the date of the auto-update. As we can see, the mozMapi32.dll has changed, but the "InUse" version wasn't updated :-( Nice mess. Richard, can you please take a look and ask someone in M-C?

summary: "Not responding" - Thunderbird frequently hangs up and doesn't allow user input after clicking on Unified Inbox. Caused by slow procssing of large list of imap UID when using "search online"
bug_report_text: I am running Thunderbird Daily on a fast i7-7700hq processor with an SSD and two email accounts -- and I frequently get Thunderbird letting me know that I can't use it (Windows says the app is not responding). I experienced similar issues in Linux as well. I don't know how to diagnose the issue further, so please advise how I can provide more information.

summary: Crash in [@ nsCopyRequest::~nsCopyRequest]

summary: Troubleshooting "Crash Reports for the Last 3 Days" shows in American date style, which differs from date format in "All Crash Reports"
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:61.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/61.0 Build ID: 20180704194937 Steps to reproduce: In Thunderbird, under Help → Troubleshooting Information there's a list of crash reports. These show the correct date style dd/mm/yy (day, month, with leading zeros). However, if you follow the link to All Crash Reports, the date format switches to m/d/yy (month, day, without leading zeros). Actual results: Date format is inconsistent. Expected results: The date format dd/mm/yy (or, perhaps, dd/mm/yyyy) should be used throughout.

summary: No sanity checking of search results for tools -> add-ons -> extensions -> find more extensions
bug_report_text: I'm using version 68.0 (build id 20190816191614) under Windows 10, with its own profile. I used tools -> add-ons -> extensions -> find more extensions to search for morefunctionsforaddressbook. I expected that to fail since it only supports up to version 60.* and its not on thunderbird.net. The search results were the Mail Merge extension. It should have returned "no results found" like it does if I tell it to search for 23skidoo. The server returned the wrong add-on. That should be reported elsewhere if it isn't already. What I'd like to report here is that there was no sanity checking of the search results. The same problem occurs with 60.8. I then searched for red. I got TORPEDO - TOoltip-poweRed Phishing Email DetectiOn (greyed out as the version is not compatible), Lightning, mail redirect, tswana spell checker, zulu spell checker, swati spell checker, tsonga spell checker, ... for 43 entries. I expected to it find Mail Redirect and RemindMeBot-Reddit (because they include the substring red in their name and claim to support the current version). If adding add-ons via tools -> add-ons is supposed to be easier/simpler than finding and downloading them using a browser .... then Thunderbird should at least check that the add-on claims to support the current version of Thunderbird and that there is a partial match of the add-ons name with what was searched for.

summary: Thunderbird stopped displaying UI on Windows 7, and process cannot be killed. Windows reports "No visible window for this process"
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/77.0.3865.120 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Since I allowed the auto updated yesterday, Thunderbird no longer shows user interface. thunderbird.exe *32 is running in background (shows in Task Manager) but does not display UI. I've tried to uninstall/reinstall, removed profile, start in safe-mode, etc. Nothing works. Cannot kill the process either. Requires reboot to remove process. Actual results: Version 68.2.1 and 68.2.0 will not display user interface. Expected results: User Interface is displayed.

summary: Thunderbird hangs deleting mail (MacOS Catilina)
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9111711 problem report with call stack User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_1) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/78.0.3904.87 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Deleting an email causes intermittent hangs on MacOS Catilina. This problem occurs at least twice per day and always with the same call stack (starting with _nextEventMatchingEventMask:untilDate:inMode:dequeue:). I have tried deleting Thunderbird preferences but that did not help. Complete call stack shown in attached file. Actual results: Hangs with beachball Expected results: Delete the email without hanging.

summary: Beta 73.0b1 address autocomplete delay when composing
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; Win64; x64; rv:72.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/72.0 Steps to reproduce: When in Sending mode, while entering recipient address, Thunderbird Beta 73.0b1 begins hanging. Actual results: May hang from 10 seconds to one minute while trying to create message in Sending mode. After recovery, it may hang two to four times before message is ready to send. Expected results: No hanging should occur when entering address characters and when typing message. With previous versions of Thunderbird up to Version 68, there was never hanging. PC is W7-64, 2TB HHD and 32Gb RAM Cached RAM averages 20Gb.

summary: JIT freezes during startup and then occasionally while using, caused by recurring event
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9370666 crash+random-freezes.txt Steps to reproduce: System: Debian 13 (x86_64), latest Thunderbird (for testing, experimental repository) Regression: worked well before 10x.x series for sure, cannot pinpoint exactly because the issue was happening before only sometimes. 1. Start Thunderbird (two accounts, after issues I setup one to manual fetching) Actual results: 2. Thunderbird is unresponsive for few seconds (sometimes survives, sometimes Waylands compositors kill it) 3. use is fine 4. freezes again in any random situation (writing message, browsing emails) I tried safe-mode with same result. Expected results: No intermittent freezes.

summary: With focus on quick filter bar or other subordinate UI elements, F6 and Shift+F6 keyboard shortcuts jump back to first/last element of fast focus ring instead of next/previous main stop in tab order
bug_report_text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/112.0

Steps to reproduce:

* Open thunderbird and
* Navigate to the folder tree by pressing tab / shift+tab or F6 / shift+F6
* Now press tab key to move the focus to one of the filter toolbar buttons e.g. view only unread messages or view starred messages.
* Now try to use F6 to move to the message list.

Or another.
* With the filter hidden press ctrl+shift+k
* Type in your filter, notice how the list filters.
* Now press F6 to jump to the message list.


Actual results:

Sometimes either F6 or shift+F6 is stuck and I need to use the other shortcut to navigate in the opposite direction.
e.g. when focus is in a filter text entry F6 does nothing and shift+F6 navigates to the message list. I may be mistaken but I'd expect F6 to move the focus to the message list and shift+F6 to move the focus into the folder tree as the filter is in the middle of the tab order when using the tab key to navigate from the folder tree to the message list.

When the focus is inside a search field shift+F6 does nothing and F6 moves focus to the folder tree.


Expected results:

I think F6 and shift+F6 should cycle F6 forward shift+F6 backward and none of them should get stuck.

summary: Thunderbird hangs when syncing carddav address book with sync token for up to 30 minutes
bug_report_text: Steps to reproduce: ### What is the issue? Thunderbird hangs in a certain situation when syncing carddav address books with a sync token. Every time the server replies with a lot of contacts this way, Thunderbird hangs while processing the reply. This behavior can be reproduced at will with Thunderbird 32bit ESR 140.0.1 to 140.6.0 - other versions (non-ESR, 64bit, older / perhaps newer) haven't been tested yet. ### How to reproduce - Windows 11 Enterprise, 64bit, newest updates - Thunderbird 140.X ESR (140.0.1 up to 140.6.0 tested), 32bit - CardDAV address book (openxchange middleware) with >15.000 contacts - added said address book to thunderbird - address book NOT fully synced - initial sync without issue, thunderbird however only receives first 1.000 contacts - reset sync-token to an older value (UNIX-Time, e.g. -1 Hour) Actual results: After the REPORT-Request sent to the CardDAV server with a sync-token is returned (return size 11MB), Thunderbird starts to hang. ProcMon reveals that millions of read entries are being done to the abook.sqlite file. On my developer-machine it takes about 2-3 minutes until the issue is resolved and thunderbird works again, on our customer PCs with far less computing power it takes up to 30 minutes. Expected results: Thunderbird shouldn't hang at all, obviously. It doesn't hang when removing the sync token from the configuration (ldap_X.servers.AddressBook.carddav.token), however every sync request from then on out would result in the server replying with the whole address book, which is something we can't afford due to our infrastructure (roughly 40.000 users hosted on openxchange).

summary: Shutdown hang in nsMsgAccountManager::CleanupOnExit, if IMAP account is configured to cleanup/expunge on exit
bug_report_text: Both Thunderbird 105.0b1 and 105.0b2 have delayed shutdown in Arch Linux compared to earlier versions. When started from the console, there is a console message when Thunderbird shuts down: [Parent 3138, Main Thread] WARNING: ContentParent: id=7f94cd9ea400 - BlockShutdown: NotifyImpendingShutdown.: file /builds/worker/checkouts/gecko/dom/ipc/ContentParent.cpp:3626 This looks connected to the similar bug referencing recent Firefox builds at bug 1783512

summary: Cannot change View Headers in Standalone Message Window
bug_report_text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/117.0

Steps to reproduce:

Right click with mouse on a message and the Open Message in New Window. Try to use View Headers All (current is View Headers Normal). I cannot select "All" by using keyboard or mouse input.

Note: View Headers All works fine when using Ctrl-O or double-clicking on a message to open it in a tab instead of in a new window.


Actual results:

Viewing Headers All did not happen. Nothing happens when All is selected. The headers view remains on Normal.


Expected results:

All headers should be displayed.

summary: Thunderbird not able to send email since upgrade 67.8.0
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.13; rv:75.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/75.0 Steps to reproduce: Reporting on behalf of elderly neighbour. User unable to send emails. Create email, and click send, box appears 'Unable to send email' no other error message; also unable to save to draft. abandoned Workaround Disbaled Windows Defender (regedit) Uninstalled Malwarebytes Uninstalled TB Reinstalled TB worked Next day - again - same happened Actual results: Workaround Disbaled Windows Defender (regedit) Uninstalled Malwarebytes Uninstalled TB Reinstalled TB worked Next day - again - same happened so uninstalled TB and it worked Expected results: Able to send email - connect to SMTP

summary: Thunderbird no longer recognizes my contacts on iMac running OSX 10.13.6
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.13; rv:61.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/61.0 Build ID: 20180807170231 Steps to reproduce: Tried to email contacts. Actual results: Contacts no longer recognized. Email addresses must be typed in individually. Names appear in red type with no contacts from iMac contact list recognized. Expected results: When typing in the first few letters of a contact's name, a drop-down contact list used to be displayed from which I could select the appropriate contact.

summary: Thunderbird immediately segfaults (arch linux, self built) because of an outdated sqlite
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9121991 gdb.txt User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:72.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/72.0 Steps to reproduce: Updated my OS (Arch linux), then try to start. I've asked other people who has updated and they don't have this issue. So it is definently a me issue. But even if I move .thunderbird to .thunderbird_bak and start it doesn't work. thunderbird --safe-mode segfaults, reinstalling doesn't work, so I'm at a loss... hope you can help, I hate having to open citrix to get a windows environment with outlook 2007.... Actual results: $ thunderbird Segmentation fault (core dumped) Expected results: Thunderbird should open.

summary: Filter changed to delete all messages older than 2 days from Inbox
bug_report_text: Yesterday (23 November) I started up my PC at abut 14:15 CET and opened up Thunderbird to read any new messages. Nothing untoward to note. About an hour later, I suddenly saw that there were only about 15 messages in the Inbox instead of the 70-odd that had been there. To cut a long story short, the culprit turned out to be an existing filter that had been edited to carry out an additional task namely to delete all messages older than 2 days from the Inbox. A check in the Activity Manager showed that 58 messages had been moved to Deleted at 15:15. Fortunately I have been able to retrieve everything and of course delete the extra task in the filter. The question is of course, how did that extra task get there? I have checked all my security aspects. There are no viruses, Trojans, etc on my system nor have there been any intrusions noted. I have also searched the internet for reports of any virus, etc that does this to no avail. Does anyone have any idea as to what could have happened?

summary: Nightly/Beta Flatpak crashes on startup if missing access to `org.kde.*` own-name
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:130.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/130.0 Steps to reproduce: I repackage the official daily tarballs in Flatpak here https://gitlab.com/projects261/thunderbird-nightly-flatpak Since tomorrow opening the Flatpak immediately crashes it. Unfortunately I could not get it to submit crash reports. To reproduce: - git clone https://gitlab.com/projects261/thunderbird-nightly-flatpak.git - git checkout b51646e4fa444f1a4d9278a5747cb6e40107fcdf - `flatpak-builder --force-clean --sandbox --user --install --install-deps-from=flathub builddir org.mozilla.ThunderbirdNightly.yaml` - flatpak run org.mozilla.ThunderbirdNightly Actual results: I think after the tray work landed, the Flatpak started to crash because it has no access to `--own-name=org.kde.*`. The tray seems to need access to `org.kde.StatusNotifierItem-$ID-$PID` but since `ID` and `PID` can change we usually have to give it access to whole of `org.kde.*`. Expected results: The lack of permission should not cause it to crash. Due to the crash there is no way to open Thunderbird without `--own-name=org.kde.*`

summary: Performance issue when message with 54,000 recipients viewed in folder.
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9289826 Dangerous to open.zip Steps to reproduce: 1. Create a new profile 2. Create a local or IMAP folder in this profile 3. Open the attached message, which has 54,000 recipients. 4. Copy the message into the folder from step 2. Actual results: Thunderbird freezes/hangs for a long time (minutes) though it will at least sometimes respond to user interaction. The hang only happens when viewing the folder from step 2. While frozen Thunderbird consumes 100% CPU. Expected results: Seems reasonable to consider this an error case but freezing is not great.

summary: Thunderbird shows very frequent "not responding" hangs after updating to MacOS High Sierra 10.13.4
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_13_4) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/67.0.3396.87 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Start Thunderbird and use it for some 3, 4, 5 minutes writing and reading email. There is no particular sequence which I can use to produce the bug but it shows up very reproducible in the sense that after having written and read a small number of emails the bug practically always shows up. Actual results: The cursor changes into the "wait" cursor and the task view shows "Thunderbird not responding". This happened on a Mac OS immediately after upgrading the OS to High Sierra and since then essentially makes Thunderbird unusable on this machine. Thunderbird has beend updated and reinstalled several times with no effect.

summary: Thunderbird closed automatically when Lightning notice calendar is displayed
bug_report_text: Hallo, Tunderbird 60.3.3 / Lightning 6.2.3.3 Windows 7 64 Bit When I start Thunderbird then in two seconds it will be tried to open the Lightning Termin info window. I can see it for an half second and then Thunderbird crashed and the Thunderbird-Exception window is displayed and Thunderbird closed automatically, Only when I deactivate Lightning (Add-On or Registry) then Thunderbird is running normally. I have many entries in this calendar. I hope somewhere can help me. I have try many Google comments. But nothing is working. THanks repeli

summary: Upgrade to TB78, UI changes on mouseover but unresponsive to mouse or keyboard. High CPU, high memory, high disk
bug_report_text: Ran the upgrade to V78 against my regular Thunderbird account. Lots of accounts, lots of folders and most of all plenty of non-functional accounts due to me not having time to follow up passwords with some providers. The install took an exceedingly long time and resulted after more than 30 minutes in a user interface that contained nothing in the folder pane, a menu bar and toolbar that did not respond to user input but did respond to mouse-overs. My notes on the upgrade process including images taken of memory use and thunderbird's appearance post upgrade can be seen here https://thunderbirdtweaks.blogspot.com/2020/07/my-experience-in-updating-to.html Bug 1625874 would be a duplicate, except I had no issues with any of the V68 releases This is a profile-specific issue I think as I have been running various versions of Daily over many months with a single account profile and no contacts or Calendars on this same device without any serious issues of the type mentioned in my upgrade to my profile. The workarounds from Bug 1625874 also did not help with this issue.

summary: Unresponsive Script warning chrome//message halting launch

Calendaring had been added but not by me, so I disabled. no extensions, and no other thing was in the configurable part. Running a 32 bit version on a x64 PC Actual results: each time it takes too long to launch and eventually stops with a chrome//message or a variant saying a script is taking too long and to continiue or stop script Expected results: Immediate launch, certainly no chrome scripts that need to be run.

summary: Email attachments display empty/blank, apparently due to unexpected URI format
bug_report_text: With TB 111 self-built daily from comm-central, any attempt to open an attached email produces only a blank window instead of showing the email. This occurs for imap when the message is in offline store or in disk cache. It also occurs when the message is in Local Folders. It appears to be due to an unexpected URI string causing the message key to not be found. For imap the following prints to the terminal console when the .eml attachment is clicked: ``` gds: uriStr = |imap://gene@wally.dbnet.lan:993/fetch%3EUID%3E.INBOX%3E555?part=1.1.3&type=application/x-message-display&filename=postacert.eml| [Parent 890880, Main Thread] WARNING: NS_ENSURE_SUCCESS(rv, rv) failed with result 0x80004005 (NS_ERROR_FAILURE): file /home/gene/mozilla2/comm/mailnews/imap/src/nsImapService.cpp:804 [Parent 890880, Main Thread] WARNING: NS_ENSURE_SUCCESS(rv, rv) failed with result 0x80004005 (NS_ERROR_FAILURE): file /home/gene/mozilla2/comm/mailnews/imap/src/nsImapService.cpp:3055 JavaScript error: chrome://messenger/content/aboutMessage.js, line 130: NS_ERROR_FAILURE: Component returned failure code: 0x80004005 (NS_ERROR_FAILURE) [nsIMsgMessageService.messageURIToMsgHdr] ``` The first line comes from my addition to nsImapUtils.cpp: ``` diff --git a/mailnews/imap/src/nsImapUtils.cpp b/mailnews/imap/src/nsImapUtils.cpp --- a/mailnews/imap/src/nsImapUtils.cpp +++ b/mailnews/imap/src/nsImapUtils.cpp @@ -34,18 +34,19 @@ nsresult nsImapURI2FullName(const char* return NS_OK; } /* parses ImapMessageURI */ nsresult nsParseImapMessageURI(const nsACString& uri, nsCString& folderURI, uint32_t* key, char** part) { if (!key) return NS_ERROR_NULL_POINTER; const nsPromiseFlatCString& uriStr = PromiseFlatCString(uri); + printf("gds: uriStr = |%s|\n", uriStr.get()); int32_t folderEnd = -1; ``` When I run with the same patch on 110 daily, there are no errors and it prints this URI: ``` imap-message://gene@wally.dbnet.lan/INBOX#555?part=1.1.3&type=message/rfc822&filename=postacert.eml ``` compared to the unexpected URI from 111: ``` imap://gene@wally.dbnet.lan:993/fetch%3EUID%3E.INBOX%3E555?part=1.1.3&type=application/x-message-display&filename=postacert.eml ``` The important difference is the "key separator" for 110 is "#" but for 111 it is "%3E". There is a similar problem with the URI when the message and .eml attachment are opened from Local Folders.

summary: Thunderbird v 60.4.0 Cannot Download Emails (POP3)
bug_report_text: Thunderbird v 60.4.0 (32-bit); 1. Click "Get Messages". On status bar "Downloading message 1 of x" appears. 2. After about 3 minutes alere "Connection to Server <server-name> was reset" appears. No messages downloaded. 3. Error Console sometimes shows nothing, sometime shows: --------------------------- window.controllers/Controllers is deprecated. Do not use it for UA detection. blank 2019-01-28 17:43:23 gloda.datastore ERROR got error in _asyncTrackerListener.handleError(): 19: constraint failed log4moz.js:680 2019-01-28 17:43:23 gloda.datastore ERROR got error in _asyncTrackerListener.handleError(): 19: constraint failed. --------------------------- Yesterday this version seemed to work fine at least once. Today it downloaded 4 messages, posted the connection reset alert, and subsequently will not download any further email. Email server is not experiencing difficulty. Thunderbird correctly states the number of messages waiting to be downloaded (as they accumulate, the number shown accurately reflects the accumulation). Running Thunderbird safe mode (add-ons disabled) has no effect on the problem. Rebooting the system has no effect. Turning off Virus scan and firewall (McAfee) has no effect. Problem does not seem to affect a separate IMAP account.
