summary: Lost/missing emails
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/84.0.4147.89 Safari/537.36 Edg/84.0.522.44 Steps to reproduce: At about 5:30pm BST (I'm in the UK) on 26 July 2020 I was about to start reading emails just after launching TB. I'd been using it earlier the same day with no known problems. Actual results: I noticed a message at the foot of the screen about deleting emails from my Sent folder. When I checked they were all gone. Then I noticed that all my Inbox emails since 19 June 2020 has also gone (older emails were still there). Many, but not all, of my other personal folders were also now empty. Folders I'd created since 19 June were no longer there. At least one of the remaining folders, with emails intact, included emails since 19 June. I then went to Virgin Media where my emails come from and found the same emails missing (I have an IMAP account). This is my main account but I have 2 other IMAP accounts but neither had any missing emails (and one had received emails since 19 June 2020). I also noticed that my main account inbox columns had changed and looked as if they'd reverted back to the original columns from when I started using TB in March 2020. Expected results: No emails should have been lost.

summary: Thunderbird crashes on startup (even in Safe Mode) every time except first startup after copying profile
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:71.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/71.0 Steps to reproduce: Problem: Thunderbird crashes on startup (even in Safe Mode) every time except first. This is a perfectly repeatable problem (at least 40 crashes so far). Platform: Intel Core 5-6600, 32 GB, Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS Thunderbird versions tested: 60.3.0, 60.9.0, 68.2.2 No extensions (that I know of) installed. I have been using Thunderbird since 2001, with the last version being 17.0.5 under Ubuntu 10.04.4 LTS, on a Pentium 4 system. I have thousands of saved emails in my profile. I am trying to upgrade to a new system under Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS. I copied the entire .thunderbird directory tree (about 6.2 GB) from the old system to a USB drive, then copied it over the .thunderbird tree from the new installation. The first time I start Thunderbird, it comes up and works perfectly. I can see all my old emails and Usenet groups, download new emails and Usenet postings, and send new emails. All is well. I then exit Thunderbird. There are no error messages or other problem indications. Sometimes I can start Thunderbird a second time and have it work perfectly, but this is rare. All subsequents attempts to start Thunderbird result in a crash. Attempts to start it with Safe Mode also crash. I have submitted many crash reports, most recently on 08-Dec-2019 (crash IDs below). There are still at least a dozen "Pending", but I don't know to send them. If I delete the .thunderbird directory tree and replace it with the saved one from the old system, it again works once (or sometimes twice), then crashes every subsequent time. I have done this under Thunderbird 60.3.0, 60.9.0, and 68.2.2 multiple times with identical results. I would like to help you find and fix this problem, but am slightly handicapped. My last programming experience was Fortran and Basic on a VAX (yes, I'm a geezer), and I don't know any modern languages. I did download the source tree for 68.2.2 but am overwhelmed by the 2 GB of 300,000 files. I don't have the foggiest idea where to start! If anyone can direct me to something like a structure tree or similar documentation, I would appreciate it. I'm afraid that my work will be limited to inserting debugging print statements at this point, but I'm willing to learn. Please let me know if there is any additional information that will help you. I will be happy to try any suggestions or tests that you think will help. The crash IDs from last Sunday are: bp-183c9b37-5955-42e7-aaba-3200c0191208.txt bp-78cac24a-e6a9-4d0d-9a44-2f6390191208.txt bp-c3c9c525-7d47-4a6c-bb01-2b9e30191208.txt bp-31cf282e-ee02-4e7a-a61f-4c4db0191208.txt bp-96bc7ba5-d82c-4a0d-bd36-f24360191208.txt bp-cbc917c4-acd7-4e78-a30d-4b99b0191208.txt bp-72341204-6b3f-4946-8996-6cedb0191208.txt bp-a3f116af-e53e-4ebe-b9a7-9d4010191208.txt bp-d1207e24-4c0b-425b-8a81-d7ddb0191208.txt bp-78afda13-88a9-4110-9879-fc1870191208.txt bp-a5020f5f-bceb-492b-8813-35d010191208.txt bp-ec8248d2-9757-445d-914a-7619c0191208.txt Thanks, Alan Actual results: Sometimes I can start Thunderbird a second time and have it work perfectly, but this is rare. All subsequents attempts to start Thunderbird result in a crash. Attempts to start it with Safe Mode also crash. Expected results: I think it should have come up and worked like previous versions.

summary: tb in background after pc wake up
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/76.0.3809.100 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Ref : bug 1573376 Thanks for your response and associated suggestions ! I already tested without add-on, it does not change the problem. I, also, recently, made a clean install of W10 -1903, and a new set up of TB (60.8) without success. The problem was already existing with previous versions of W10. Nevertheless, the problem never happened in the past, and only appeared 6-12 months ago. The problem is present in 90% of the cases when the PC has been in standby for a few hours, almost never if the standby was a few tens of minutes (although I turned off the long term standby) If I maintain TB on the screen before the PC is going to standby, the problem does not appear, TB is responsive at the wake up. Right now, I did not test the beta version, but I don't really believe in an improvement on that point. I have no icon present near the clock Thanks in advance for your investigations

summary: Thunderbird fails working after upgrade to rev 60.7.2
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/75.0.3770.100 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: On my laptop with win7pro I installed the actual Thunderbird version 60.7.2 as recommended (before rev. 60.7.0. was installed). After this Thunderbird was not able to access the internet any more and thus to download or upload emails. Actual results: Trying an older test version of thunderbird (60.5.?) all worked properly as before. On the same laptop using win10 the new Thunderbird rev 60.7.2 worked perfectly as well. On win7 I restored my older rev. 60.7.0 and now all worked well as before using the same account data. Expected results: My conclusion is that 60.7.2 has a problem on win7 accessing the internet and thus is not usable for me any more. Assuming that this failure might penetrate into newer versions of TB I guess I cannot install new versions of TB any more.

summary: Thunderbird Crash deleting a folder in [@ EMPTY: no frame data available; MissingThreadList]
bug_report_text: On Mac with a try build based on beta 118, I reproduced twice. * In account settings > copies and folders > Set archive options to `keep existing folder structure` * create F1 as a subfolder of account and F2 as subfolder of F1, put a message in each * visit F1 and archive a message * F1 appears as a subfolder of Archives * attempt to delete F1 under Archives Crash There may be simpler steps - I haven't attempted to simply. summary: Error Message from autoconfig  keeps being added to Welcome Screen
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9011908 Screenshot User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:60.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/60.0 Build ID: 20100101 Steps to reproduce: Start Thunderbird without network access and create a new profile: sudo unshare -n sudo -u damon thunderbird -P (Note that "Go Offline" was not checked in the profile dialog, network access is denied by "unshare") OS: Ubuntu 18.04.1 Arch: x86_64 Package version: 52.9.1+build3-0ubuntu0.18.04.1 Actual results: In the "Welcome to Thunderbird" window that appears when starting with a blank profile, the following error message appears in red font (see attachment): Sorry - we're unable to communicate with our sign-up server. Please check your connection. The error message is endlessly repeated (appended) every 5 seconds, filling the window. Scrolling down is still possible. Meanwhile, the terminal from which Thunderbird was launched shows a similar error message: 2018-09-25 21:03:39 mail.provider ERROR Something went wrong loading the provider list JSON file. Going into offline mode. Expected results: The error message in the GUI window should be printed once, subsequent error messages should overwrite the existing message instead of being appended to the text in the welcome window.

summary: Archive button on a collapsed thread of messages doesn't work
bug_report_text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:111.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/111.0

Steps to reproduce:

Tested in 112.0b3 (64-bit), Linux

IMAP inbox with two threaded messages.  Collapse and select the thread.  Click the "Archive" button.


Actual results:

Button visually depresses, but nothing happens.


Expected results:

Both emails in the thread should be archived.

Note: Instead of clicking the Archive button, choosing Menu -> Message -> Archive works as expected.  As does clicking the Archive button on expanded messages in the same thread.

summary: Thunderbird error message: "You've launched an older version of Firefox - Using an older version of Firefox can corrupt bookmarks..."
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9050757 TB with FF error message Thunderbird should never claim to be Firefox... that's a bug. See attached error message. I installed latest Daily 67.0a1 (2019-03-09) (64-bit) which apparently marked my existing test profile as upgraded and incompatible with any other older version of TB. Upon starting a slightly older version of TB Daily (maybe 3 wks old), it now rejects to start with that profile - perhaps helpful, but very annoying. Worse, the error message is all about Firefox - which leads me to suspect that maybe TB may not need to be so strict about it? I've always shared my test profile with all sorts of different TB versions and never experienced any problem. Any workarounds would also be welcome, can I change a pref to force it?

summary: Unable to use existing profile (-p --allow-downgrade) TB68 beta 3 build 3
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:69.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/69.0 Steps to reproduce: Installed Beta 3 Build 3. Started with -p --allow-downgrade Actual results: Profile manager starts - I choose existing profile but it is not used and no accounts show up - instead it offers to create an account as if installing for the first time. This is pretty bad guys. I use 7-10 accounts and encryption - setting up all the accounts every time is a ridiculous burden. Expected results: Existing profile should have been used. It is not practical to use the beta version - please fix so testing can resume.

summary: Richard crash without crash report generated [meta]
bug_report_text: Following upgrade to Thunderbird 74.0b1 (32-bit) it has crashed unexpectedly 3-4 time this week without generating crash report or proposing to report the issue... I had to manually start application again. When if first occurred, I thought I had perhaps closed it by mistake, but last it happens (just now) it was after clicking the Send button to send a message... previously I was not doing anything in particular in TB, it just seem to crash by itself... It is probably expected from a b1 version but just thought to mention it... After a manual start of TB, the message appeared send and available in Sent items folder... on the server (IMAP/SMTP account).

summary: Thunderbird freezing regularly with "not responding" message due to "transition from sleep to hibernate"
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9434286 Why is Thunderbird persistently not responding.txt User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/130.0.0.0 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Please see the attached file... 30 Oct 2024 Why is Thunderbird persistently "not responding"? This problem has become steadily worse over the last several months, to the extent that it has been making me think that I need to move to a different email client. There may be some clues in https://support.mozilla.org/eu/questions/1446914 I think I have seen other apps exhibit this "not responding" symptom as well. But I use Thunderbird many times each day, and the problem has been getting worse for the last month or so with Thunderbird. The link above suggests the problem may be caused by Windows Defender repeatedly scanning the Thunderbird InBox. Whatever the cause, the cooling fan indicates increased CPU loading but it doesn't show up on Task Manager. However I think I have found a way to avoid the problem. Not the same as fix it however. Several months ago, probably following one of the monthly Microsoft updates, my Win 10 laptop which was set to sleep after 30 minutes inactivity, began transitioning to hibernate after roughly 3 hours in sleep mode. On awakening in the morning, it was essentially busy with housekeeping for at least 5 minutes, unlike awakening from sleep which left it usable after perhaps 30 seconds. An annoyance, but bearable. And seemingly not long after this, but not immediately, the problem with Thunderbird freezing with a "not responding" message showed up. At first it seemed occasional. But by this week it was happening after I looked at every 3 or 4 messages, and the not-responding delay was often 30 seconds or more. Extremely annoying. And this after I had taken steps to keep the InBox down to perhaps the last couple days of mail (which seemingly made absolutely no difference). I got curious about the postings about Windows Defender scanning and re-scanning email. It's enabled (plus MalwareBytes) on my laptop but I don't know anything about it. So I decided to look into whether there might be a relationship with the unexpected transition from sleep to hibernate, which I definitely had not enabled. Didn't take long to find out online that "hybrid sleep" is becoming commonplace for laptops, to extend battery life I suppose. I was unaware of it, and don't think the setting has historically been available. I discovered that a transition from sleep to hibernate was set for 180 minutes and I am absolutely certain that I didn't make this setting. So I set the transition to 1440 hours (1 day) since it is almost a given that I will be using the laptop every day. Plus I didn't know whether "never" was a valid entry for the transition time although the window would accept that word. I also ensured that hybrid sleep was off for both battery and plugged-in modes (it was already). So at least in theory there is no connection with a hybrid power management setting since it was already disabled. BOTTOM LINE: The unexplained problem with Thunderbird posting a "not responding" message and freezing for 30 seconds or so, HAS DISAPPEARED. So it seems clear that I was blaming Thunderbird while the problem has a different cause. Will the problem stay gone? I don't know. But if you are receiving other complaints about Thunderbird annoying laptop users with a "not responding" message, this note might point you in a useful direction for a fix.

summary: After auto-upgrade "thunderbird.exe is not a valid win32 application"
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux i686) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/57.0.2987.98 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Nothing (auto-upgrade, no pro-action). The OS is an old 32 bit XP. Actual results: The following message comes up when trying to launch the .exe: "C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird\thunderbird.exe is not a valid win32 application" Same happens when trying to launch any *.exe from the C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird\ Expected results: Thunderbird seem to pass to a 64bit version ? Reinstalling by downloading the last installer fixes the issue. I found a few dozen similar problem accross the web with the same issue. (https://duckduckgo.com/lite?q=%22thunderbird.exe+is+not+a+valid+win32+application%22)

summary: When receiving an email fails, Thunderbird stops receiving following emails.
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; Win64; x64; rv:62.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/62.0 Build ID: 20180830143136 Steps to reproduce: I’ve got an email in aol.com with big attachments and (for example) 3 newer emails. Thunderbird starts working as usual and put the message, that there are 4 emails to receive. Receiving the first email failed. Then Thunderbird didn’t try to receive the following emails and put the message, that there are no more emails. From the web-interface of aol.com the email with the big attachments is correct and readable. I moved this email with the web-interface from the input folder into another folder on which Thunderbird has no access. Then Thunderbird was able to receive the 3 newer emails. I moved this email with the web-interface back to the input folder. Then didn’t receive following emails again. The bug is reproducible. Actual results: I’ve got an email in aol.com with big attachments and (for example) 3 newer emails. Thunderbird starts working as usual and put the message, that there are 4 emails to receive. Receiving the first email failed. Then Thunderbird didn’t try to receive the following emails and put the message, that there are no more emails. Expected results: Thunderbird should give a message that receiving of one email failed. And Thunderbird should continue receiving the following emails.

summary: Losing my view locations with SeaMonkey v2.49.4's multiple tabs for e-mails...
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; rv:52.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/52.0 SeaMonkey/2.49.4 Steps to reproduce: 1. Have multiple downloaded e-mails. 2. Read one and scroll to its bottom. 3. Open a new tab in and read another e-mail. 4. Go back to the original tab with its original e-mail. Actual results: It goes back to the beginning of the e-mail instead of the bottom. Expected results: It should have taken me where I left off. It's annoying if I need to go back and forth. I was told Thunderbird has this problem too.

summary: Scan to email function broke with 60.5.2
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_13_6) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/12.0.3 Safari/605.1.15 Steps to reproduce: On Windows 10 had previously setup scan to email function from Brother and HP printer/scanner. The steps are: (1) scan a document from either Brother or HP printer/scanner selecting the option to scan to email. The paper would feed through the scanner in an expected fashion. Actual results: The scan opens Thunderbird then an error message appears "another application is attempting to send mail using your user profile". From here I could not continue, only close the error message. After I close I am back at Thunderbird at the screen that was open before (in my case the Inbox). Expected results: On 60.5.1 and recent earlier versions using scan to email option would scan as expected. Shortly after the scan would finished a Thunderbird "Write" screen would open with the scan attached.

summary: Web Contents such as OAuth login page doesn't work with screen readers.
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/97.0.4692.71 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Open something that display web page like OAuth login page or rss feed. Try to access with screen reader such as Narrator, NVDA, or Jaws. Actual results: It just says "NetscapeDispatchWnD." Expected results: It should read actual contents of the page.
